**BYLAWS**

**DELAWARE ASSOCIATION OF DISABILITY EXAMINERS**

**ARTICLE I**

**NAME**

The name of this organization shall be the Delaware Association of

Disability Examiners (DADE).

**ARTICLE II**

**AFFILIATION**

The Delaware Association of Disability Examiners is a state chapter of the

National Association of Disability Examiners.

**ARTICLE III**

**DEFINITIONS**

1. Association: The National Association of Disability Examiners.

2. Region: the Mid-Atlantic Region, including those states so designated by the

Association.

3. Regional Chapter: the Mid-Atlantic Region Association of Disability

Examiners.

4. Chapter: the Delaware Association of Disability Examiners.

**ARTICLE IV**

**PURPOSE**

1. To advance the profession of disability insurance claims adjudication.

2. To promote the quality of service rendered to the public by the profession.

3. To provide a forum for members’ concerns.

4. To provide a medium for social and professional interaction among members.

**ARTICLE V**

**MEMBERSHIP**

Association members residing or working within the state of Delaware shall constitute

the membership.

**ARTICLE VI**

**FINANCES AND MEMBERSHIP YEAR**

1. Dues. There shall be no dues except those for Association membership.

2. Membership year. The membership year shall coincide with that of the

Association.

**ARTICLE VII**

**MEMBERSHIP MEETINGS**

There shall be an annual business meeting at a time and place to be determined by the

Executive board. Additionally, the president may call interim meetings as necessary and shall call a meeting upon request of a majority of the board members.

**ARTICLE VIII**

**ELECTION**

At the annual business meeting, to be held prior to July 1, the membership shall elect its officers: the regional board members, in alternate years; the nominations committee chairperson; and one board member at large. Voting will be by secret ballot. Nominations may be accepted from the floor.

**ARTICLE IX**

**OFFICERS**

1. Designated. The officers shall be a president, historian, secretary and treasurer.

2. Terms. The officers shall serve terms of two years or until their successors are selected and may succeed themselves.

3. Duties. The officers shall perform the duties prescribed by those bylaws and by the parliamentary authority or any other rules adopted by the executive board or membership.

**ARTICLE X**

**REGIONAL BOARD MEMBER**

The chapter shall elect one member to the regional chapter board, to serve two years or until a successor is selected, the term to begin at the conclusion of the next regional delegate assembly.

**ARTICLE XI**

**EXECUTIVE BOARD**

1. Composition. The executive board shall consist of the officers, the immediate past president, regional board members and one member at large.

2. Meetings. The executive board shall meet on the call of the president. Additionally, a meeting shall be called on the request of a majority of the board members.

3. Duties. The executive board shall conduct the affairs of the chapter between meetings of the membership. The board shall have the authority to:

a. adopt a budget and expend funds from the treasury;

b. fix the time and place of meetings of the membership;

c. fill by its own vote, offices or board positions which have become vacant

between elections;

d. act upon reports of committees.

4. Quorum. A majority of the members of the board shall constitute a quorum.

**ARTICLE XII**

**COMMITTEES**

1. Standing. At the annual business meeting, the incoming president shall announce, subject to confirmation by those present and voting, the chairpersons of the following committees, except for the nominations committee:

a. Membership. Presently the division director is a proponent of NADE/DADE and the agency pays for the annual dues of its members, however; in the event this policy changes, this committee will be activated.

b. Program. Any chapter member shall recommend to the executive board any suggestions for chapter activities. The chair person for any activity shall be selected at a chapter meeting. The chairperson shall also be available to serve on the regional program committee.

c. Nominations. The position of Nominations chairperson will be held by the at large board member. The chairperson shall seek candidates for each elective position to be filled at the next election. The chairperson shall also be available to serve on the regional nominations committee.

2. Ad hoc. The president may also appoint, with executive board approval, any special

committees as necessary.

**ARTICLE XIII**

**PARLIAMENTARY AUTHORITY**

The chapter shall be governed by the latest edition of Robert’s Rules of Order, insofar as they do not conflict with these bylaws or any rules adopted by the executive board or membership.

**ARTICLE XIV**

**AMENDMENTS**

1. An amendment may be proposed by a majority of the executive board and ratified by two-thirds of the members present and voting at any membership meeting.

2. An amendment proposed at a membership meeting and approved by a majority of members shall be referred for study to an ad hoc committee. Recommendations of this committee shall be heard at the next meeting of the members where the amendment may be ratified by a two-thirds vote.
